name: Toba Batak

ancestors.notes: Northwest Sumatra–Barrier Islands / Batak branch of Malayo-Polynesian.

notes: Toba Batak (Hata Batak Toba), North Sumatra, Indonesia. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Wikidata records the indigenous Batak script (Surat Batak), an abugida with an inherent vowel; in practice Toba Batak is today written in the Latin alphabet, and a Latin-orthography map (not attempted here) would be the practical input contract. No grapheme map is claimed for either script. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode bata1289), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode bata1289.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Batak Toba.
